primary_function: "Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation."

authoritative: DOCX file creation with docx-js library
DOCX file editing and XML manipulation
Document conversion between formats (.doc to .docx, DOCX to PDF/images)
Text extraction and content analysis from DOCX files
Page layout configuration and sizing (US Letter, A4, landscape/portrait)
Typography and styling (fonts, headings, paragraph formatting)
List formatting with proper numbering systems

suggested_aec_gates: Verify page size is explicitly set (never rely on A4 default for US documents)
Confirm tables use WidthType.DXA (never WidthType.PERCENTAGE for Google Docs compatibility)
Ensure tables have both columnWidths and individual cell width properties
Validate lists use LevelFormat.BULLET/DECIMAL (never unicode bullet characters)
Confirm ShadingType.CLEAR is used for table cell backgrounds (not SOLID)
Verify images include required type parameter and altText properties
Ensure PageBreak elements are wrapped inside Paragraph containers
Confirm Arial font is specified as default (universal compatibility)
Validate document structure includes proper validation step after creation
